cowardice_flags.avoided: Any explanation for why total direct losses fell 31.8% or why public infrastructure's share jumped to 60% despite documented flood events in 2025.
cowardice_flags.should_say: The drop in household/business losses masks persistent structural failures in flood prevention; public assets now absorb the majority because mitigation infrastructure and land-use planning remain chronically inadequate across administrations.

cowardice_flags.avoided: No mention of low insurance uptake or indirect costs explicitly called out.
cowardice_flags.should_say: Only ~5% of flood victims had insurance; the falling household bill reflects limited private coverage and possibly one-off factors rather than systemic resilience.

audience_check: A taxi driver in Ipoh, teacher in Kota Bharu, hawker in Penang, farmer in Sabah or factory worker in Johor would tune out or roll their eyes — reads like an academic exercise in methodology and balance-sheet accounting that never speaks directly to their lived reality of repeated annual flooding.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Decades of flood mitigation funding under successive BN, PH and PN governments have failed to break the cycle because of fragmented responsibilities, poor enforcement of land-use rules and priority on short-term infrastructure over long-term resilience.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Directly implicates governance failure across coalitions without naming ministers; violates process-not-substance rule.

hard_truths_missing.truth: East-coast states (Kelantan, Terengganu) — overwhelmingly Malay and rural — consistently record highest flood losses; poverty, agricultural dependence and settlement patterns in floodplains amplify vulnerability.
Low-income rural and semi-urban households bear disproportionate unquantified indirect costs while 'public assets' category socialises the bill onto all taxpayers.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Shifting focus to 'mix' without class lens keeps narrative race-neutral.

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Women shoulder post-flood unpaid labour (cleaning, childcare, family health) — ignored by direct-damage statistics.

class_truths_avoided.truth: 50.6% household drop hides that lower-class families in flood-prone areas have fewer resources to relocate/insure/rebuild; rising public share effectively subsidises repeated exposure rather than preventing it.
class_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Reveals racial framing often masks deeper class-based vulnerability.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: Late 2026 or early 2027 monsoon will again displace tens of thousands and push public infrastructure repair costs above RM400M while indirect losses spike unemployment and food prices.
predictions.who_can_fix: NADMA, DID, state governments, Ministry of Works through integrated land-use reform and mandatory private-sector risk financing.
predictions.why_they_wont: Short electoral cycles, developer lobbying on floodplains, inter-agency buck-passing.
predictions.label: HYPOTHESIS

cardinal_sin_flags.in_my_attack: Claiming only 'governance issues' without citing specific Auditor-General findings on wasted flood projects
cardinal_sin_flags.why_I_dropped_it: Could not independently locate 2025-specific primary AG report URL.

strongest_card.why: Diplomatic cowardice lands hardest because the analysis itself admits 'no causal certainty' and 'process-not-substance' while the verified DOSM data and World Bank projections scream for exactly the causal/governance discussion it evades.

weakest_card.why: Testable predictions are necessarily hypothetical; without new primary data on 2026 monsoon outcomes, they remain informed speculation rather than sourced attacks.

overall_assessment: Competent number-crunching dressed up as insight — accurate but deliberately gutless. It reports the smaller bill and the shifted burden without ever asking why Malaysia keeps flooding the same way year after year or who keeps failing to fix it. Sterile accounting that lets every political actor off the hook while ordinary Malaysians in Kelantan, Terengganu and Johor keep paying. Courage without substance is just statistics.
